benchmark_details.name: InvisibleBench
benchmark_details.overview: Evaluates raw conversational models on caregiver-support safety using a shared public scenario corpus and gate-plus-quality scoring.

purpose_and_intended_users.task_description: Multi-turn caregiver-support evaluation for raw LLMs.
purpose_and_intended_users.goal: Assess safety-aligned behavior for caregiver support across short and long interactions.
out_of_scope_uses: public comparison of experimental system harnesses
limitations: Scenario coverage is curated and cannot represent all caregiving contexts.
Confidential holdout scenarios are private and not part of the public repo.

The published Safety claim surface is calibration-gated; per-mode gold sets are validated for 4 of 50 checks (hard-fail Safety/Scope decisions match the resolved 60-trace human gold set, 60/60, kappa 1.0). The remaining checks are provisional and excluded from the claim surface.

The current leaderboard covers 11 models x 63 scenarios (v3.1 Phase 2). Phase 2 roster: Claude Opus 4.7, GPT-5.5, GPT-5 Mini, Gemini 3.1 Pro, Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite, Grok 4.3, DeepSeek V4 Pro, GLM-5.1, MiniMax M2.7, Gemma 4 31B, Nemotron 3 Super 120B.
Care distributions are directional and provisional, never merged with Safety, until quality-layer human calibration is expanded.

categories.focus: crisis detection, boundaries, adversarial pressure (includes 4 contrast-set variants)

formats: scenario JSON with persona, turns, metadata, scoring_dimensions
dataset_characteristics.confidential_handling: Confidential holdout scenarios are private inputs loaded from an external directory and excluded from the public repo and public leaderboard.

methods: Safety/Care v1 scoring with per-check verifiers (no composite, no rank)
50 checks across 9 dimensions (4 Safety lines + 5 Care qualities)
metrics: safety.violation_rate (per line: crisis, scope, identity, autonomy)
methodology.composite: none — Safety and Care are reported side by side; no weighting and no overall_score (retired A/B/C/D/F aliases: see docs/ontology.md old -> new map)
methodology.calculation: Safety/Care v1 scoring with per-check verifiers — no composite, no rank. Safety: 4 lines (Crisis, Scope, Identity, Autonomy) reported as per-line violation rates (claim-bearing, calibration-gated). Care: 5 qualities (Belonging, Attunement, Trauma-awareness, Relational, Advocacy) reported as directional, provisional distributions, never merged with Safety. 50 checks across 9 dimensions with regex, LLM, and corpus verifiers.
methodology.interpretation: Lower Safety violation rates indicate safer behavior on each line; the published claim surface is calibration-gated. Care distributions are directional and provisional — read as patterns, not verdicts. Output is a per-model jagged safety profile, not a rank. Blindspot profiles surface per-check failure patterns across the fleet.
methodology.validation: v3.1: public hard-fail Safety decisions match the resolved 60-trace human gold set at 60/60 (kappa 1.0). Per-mode gold sets validated for 4 of 50 checks (IB-A1, IB-A8, IB-F3, IB-B1 seed); remaining checks provisional and excluded from the published claim surface. Phase 2 covers 11 models x 63 scenarios with 4 residual UNCLEAR verdicts (quality-layer, fail-closed).

ethical_and_legal_considerations.privacy_and_anonymity: Scenarios are curated; avoid logging sensitive caregiver data in evaluation transcripts.
ethical_and_legal_considerations.consent_procedures: Not applicable for synthetic or curated scenarios.
ethical_and_legal_considerations.compliance_with_regulations: Jurisdictional rules are encoded in benchmark/configs/rules for compliance checks.
ethical_considerations: Do not publish private confidential scenarios or results from them.
Results are for evaluation and should not be used as clinical guidance.
Benchmark overfitting can reduce real-world safety robustness.
